title: Custom mapped type — {[K in keyof T]: T[K] | null}
description: A user-authored mapped type that augments every prop with `| null`. Tests that resolver + emit thread custom mapped types correctly; Partial / Required / Pick etc. exercise the same machinery via the built-in utility paths.
notes: Each prop resolves to a `T[K] | null` union, so `null` is accepted at every prop but a missing prop still FAILS — the props remain required (mapping adds `| null`, not optionality).
schema: createValidate(RT.object({a: RT.union([RT.string(), RT.literal(null)]), b: RT.union([RT.number(), RT.literal(null)])}))
generated: function YOw_yWoI8CK(v){return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && (v.a === null || typeof v.a === 'string') && (v.b === null || Number.isFinite(v.b)))}
